author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
## Findings

D3b (in `sub4-contractdrift.sh`, added 2026-06-26 followup to c67f162) reproduced the push-rejected envelope via the `git clone + jj git init --colocate + jjf pull` fallback recipe — closing the harness gap where the original D3's `cp -R` left both clones sharing jj's op-log so no divergence occurred. Full snapshot in `experiments/qa-redteam-2026-06-25/.scratch/d3b/evidence/d3b-hint-snapshot.txt`.

D3b's substring probe confirmed substring-matchable phrases in the `message` field:

- `"retry"`
- `"rejected"`
- `"fetch first"` (raw libgit2 marker — version-dependent)
- `` "run `jjf pull" `` (the appended jjforge advisory)

This matches the contract-drift call-out in this ticket's body and validates the proposed fix (move `hint` into `details.hint` to mirror the `concurrent_write` shape; structured `details.refs_rejected` to obsolete the raw refspec leak).

## Recommendation

No new ticket. This comment is the cross-link from the 2026-06-26 followup to the existing finding. The D3b harness is the durable repro for whoever picks this up.

## Confidence

high. Reproduced from a clean scratch repo and snapshot archived; behavior matches the ticket body verbatim.

## Open follow-ups

- none.
